title: Mold — discard starter
summary: Visible mold means do not rescue the starter. Start again safely.
steps: Wash and sanitize the jar before restart.
warnings: Mold is a food safety issue.
dontDo: do not remove only top layer
do not bake with moldy starter

title: Mold — safe restart
steps: Start new starter with small flour and water portion.
Keep jar edges clean and feed regularly.
warnings: There is no safe home MVP method to rescue moldy starter.
dontDo: do not bake with mold
do not ignore colored growth

title: Rotten smell — discard starter
summary: Rotten, musty or suspicious smell means stop using it.
steps: Do not use it in dough.
Start again in a clean jar.
warnings: Acidic or mild alcohol smell can be normal; rotten smell is not.
dontDo: do not hide smell by feeding
do not add it to bread

title: Rotten smell — safe restart
summary: Decision plan when starter should not be saved.
steps: Start with fresh flour and water.
warnings: Safety is more important than saving flour.
dontDo: do not taste suspicious starter
do not keep feeding spoiled starter

title: Starter not rising — first step
summary: Often caused by low temperature, weak starter or feeding rhythm.
warnings: Young starter may need several steady days.
dontDo: do not add yeast to starter
do not overheat jar

title: Starter not rising — rescue plan
steps: Keep 20 g starter and discard the rest.
Add 20 g water and 20 g flour.
Use only after clear rise and pleasant sour smell.
warnings: If mold or rotten smell appears, discard starter.
dontDo: do not feed huge amount of weak starter
do not bake with inactive starter

title: Dough not rising — check time and temperature
summary: Sourdough often needs more time than yeast dough.
steps: Watch volume and bubbles, not only the clock.
warnings: Cold kitchen can slow fermentation a lot.
dontDo: do not bake dough that did not move
do not overheat dough

title: Dough not rising — full plan
summary: Correct rise by checking starter, temperature and time.
steps: Check if starter doubled before mixing.
Move bowl to stable warmer place if cold.
Next bake use more active starter or longer fermentation.
warnings: Do not fix weak dough by aggressive heating.
dontDo: do not judge only by clock
do not add lots of flour late

title: Dense crumb
summary: Can come from short fermentation, weak starter, too much water or cutting hot bread.
steps: Next time use active starter or longer fermentation.
warnings: Hot bread can look gummy after cutting.
dontDo: do not cut hot bread
do not increase hydration too fast

title: Dense crumb — correction plan
steps: Compare fermentation time with room temperature.
Lower water next time if dough was too wet.
Extend fermentation if dough had no bubbles.
Change only one variable next bake.
warnings: One failed loaf does not mean bad starter.
dontDo: do not change everything at once
do not judge hot bread

title: Pale crust — first step
summary: Usually temperature, short bake or not enough early steam.
steps: Preheat pot or tray well next time.
warnings: Do not overbake endlessly if inside is already dry.
dontDo: do not open oven too often

title: Pale crust — correction plan
summary: Improve crust color with heat, steam and time.
steps: Use covered pot or steam if safe.
warnings: Be careful with hot pot and steam.
dontDo: do not pour water onto unsafe glass
do not handle hot pot barehanded

title: Bread cracking — basic correction
summary: Cracking can mean short proof, no scoring or dry surface.
steps: Give final proof a bit more time.
warnings: Controlled split at scoring is normal.
dontDo: do not bake tight loaf without scoring

title: Bread cracking — full plan
summary: Correct cracking through scoring, proofing and moisture.
steps: If side cracks appear, extend final proof.
Use steam or covered pot if safe.
dontDo: do not score with dull knife
do not leave loaf uncovered too long

title: Feeding schedule — simple start
summary: Regular small feeding is better than keeping too much starter.
steps: Feed equal weights flour and water.
warnings: Fridge starter is slower but still needs feeding.
dontDo: do not keep adding food to a huge amount

title: Feeding schedule — plan
summary: Simple plan for active and fridge starter.
steps: Feed before baking and wait for visible rise.
In fridge feed less often but observe smell and look.
warnings: If mold or rotten smell appears, discard starter.
dontDo: do not bake with inactive starter
do not ignore mold

title: Basic sourdough diagnosis
summary: Write stage, temperature, time and smell before changing recipe.
warnings: Mold or rotten smell means discard starter.
dontDo: do not fix everything at once

title: Universal sourdough correction plan
summary: Calm method for improving next bake.
steps: Name issue: starter, dough, crumb, crust or cracking.
warnings: The app gives home guidance and only simple food-safety warnings.
dontDo: do not use moldy starter
do not change everything at once
